headline: Ford Is in Talks With China’s Geely. Why the ‘Most American’ Auto Maker Is Looking Abroad.
summary: Ford Motor  the most American car maker, is thinking about going Chinese.  The Wall Street Journal reported Friday that Ford Motor held talks with Chinese auto maker Geely that could result in Geely technology in the U.S.  Ford didn’t immediately respond to a request for comment.

raw.headline: Ford Is in Talks With China’s Geely. Why the ‘Most American’ Auto Maker Is Looking Abroad.

headline: Ford, Geely explored extending European partnership to U.S.
summary: Investing.com -- Ford Motor and Chinese automaker Geely held talks earlier this year about potentially extending their European partnership negotiations to the U.S., according to a Friday report by the Wall Street Journal.

raw.headline: Ford, Geely explored extending European partnership to U.S.

headline: Is Rivian’s R2 The Pivot That Could Reprice The Stock?
summary: On April 22, 2026, Rivian (RIVN) commenced customer production of the R2 SUV in Normal, Illinois. The pricey R1 series established the brand. The R2 will determine the business.

headline: Our robotaxi safety record will be key to profitability: WeRide CEO
summary: WeRide CEO Tony Han says the reliable performance of its robotaxis will gain the trust of local regulators that will help drive the company's profitability. He also discusses Genesis, WeRide’s AI simulation platform, noting its potential for broader use across robotic applications.
